extension.summary: Experiment: no-blip OPPO playback with pure, spec-legitimate HDMI-CEC switching (no IR, no spoofing).
extension.description: OppoKodiBridge v4 is a fork of OppoKodiBridge v3 that keeps v3's playercorefactory handoff (Kodi routes disc content to an external OPPO player, so there is no pre-play "blip") but switches the TV using ONLY legitimate HDMI-CEC. The OPPO grabs its HDMI input via its OWN One-Touch-Play, forced by power-cycling the OPPO (it only asserts active source on a power-ON transition); Kodi reclaims its input by re-asserting its OWN active source. There is deliberately NO IR blaster and NO CEC injection / spoofed initiator (which corrupts the shared bus). The trade-off is the ~20-24s OPPO power-cycle on every handoff, accepted in exchange for zero extra hardware and a clean bus. Installs alongside v2 and v3 (different add-on id) for direct comparison. The OPPO HTTP API is community-reverse-engineered, not an official protocol.
extension.news: 4.1.1 -- Bug-sweep fixes (adversarially audited; follow-up to 4.1.0). (1) The M9205 verbose #SVM 3 stop-watch could spin forever holding the :23 socket when the device streamed chatter without a recognised @UPL stop token -- the prime suspect for the sluggish/locked IR remote -- because its HTTP idle cross-check fired only while the push stream was quiet and it had no absolute time ceiling; the watch now runs the HTTP cross-check on a wall-clock cadence (ending after idle_confirmations consecutive confirmed-idle reads, so a transient blip never causes a premature mid-playback reclaim) and enforces a max_watch_seconds ceiling. (2) A loose .bdmv index file NOT under a BDMV/ folder caused the handoff to NFS-mount the .bdmv FILE's own path (which hard-crashes the OPPO) and play the bare mount root; disc_folder now returns the directory that CONTAINS the index file. (3) Mount/play success checks used identity 'is False', which missed loosely-typed non-bool device replies (0 / "false"); added reply_failed() coercion at all three sites. (4) The playercorefactory rule now also matches a root-level BDMV/VIDEO_TS segment, in lockstep with the runtime classifier. 90 off-box tests green; each fix independently re-audited. SOFTWARE-VERIFIED ONLY (hardware validation pending). 4.1.0 -- Reference-aligned the play + monitor paths (to the proven emby-chinoppo-bridge) and simplified model handling. ISO now sends STP + ~4s settle before /playnormalfile (previously sent before BDMV); BDMV/VIDEO_TS sends NO STP and starts via /checkfolderhasBDMV, with a /playnormalfile fallback if that reports failure. Stop detection is now model-aware: the M9207 Plus / UDP-203 is watched over HTTP /getglobalinfo ONLY and never opens the #SVM 3 verbose socket on :23 (the prime suspect for that clone's sluggish/locked IR remote during playback); the M9205 keeps the instant #SVM 3 push watch (+ HTTP fallback). Dropped the M9207 export-root/sub-path NFS mode -- ALL models now mount the file's folder and play the bare leaf (the M9205/reference layout), and oppo_model only selects the stop-monitor transport now. 81 off-box tests green, each change independently audited. SOFTWARE-VERIFIED ONLY (not yet validated on hardware); the M9207 NFS change reverses 0.4.3 -- if disc/ISO playback is rejected at the mount step on a UDP-203 clone, the previous export-root layout can be restored from git history. 4.0.0 -- Renamed the add-on from v3 to v4: new id service.oppokodibridge.v4 and name "OppoKodiBridge v4 (CEC)", so it installs as a distinct add-on alongside v2 and v3 for direct A/B/C comparison. Carries over the pure-CEC handoff pipeline (detector/handoff/cec/monitor/orchestrator) from 3.1.0; also scopes disc detection to ISO/BDMV/VIDEO_TS and now DERIVES the playercorefactory routing rules from the same constants the runtime classifier uses, so the generated XML and is_handoff_target cannot drift apart (HD-DVD/HVDVD_TS is left to Kodi -- the OPPO cannot play it anyway). 75 off-box tests green. 3.1.0 -- OppoKodiBridge v3 adopts pure HDMI-CEC switching. v3.0.0 switched the TV with a Broadlink IR blaster; this release replaces that with legitimate CEC (the OPPO grabs its HDMI input via its OWN One-Touch-Play on a #POF->#PON power-cycle; Kodi reclaims via libCEC SetActiveSource -- no IR, no CEC injection) and brings the modular detector/handoff/cec/monitor/orchestrator pipeline with 73 off-box tests. It defaults oppo_model to M9205 (mount the movie's OWN folder + play the bare filename) -- the NFS layout that lets disc-image .iso files loop-mount to /mnt/iso and play, verified live. The OPPO HTTP API is community-reverse-engineered, not an official protocol. 0.4.5 -- Fix the in-add-on Setup & tests buttons (Ping / Control test / CEC switch-over). They all crashed before doing anything with "No valid addon id could be obtained" -- config.from_addon() called xbmcaddon.Addon() with no id, which Kodi can't resolve when a script is launched via RunScript (only the background service runs "in a normal Kodi manner"). Now passes the explicit addon id; the test fake was hardened to catch this class of bug. 0.4.4 -- Corrected the M9207 grab (hardware-tested live). The oppo_model setting now ONLY selects the NFS playback layout (M9205 = mount the file's folder + bare name; M9207 Plus / UDP-203 = mount the export ROOT + sub-path -- the part verified to work). The TV grab is the plain #POF -> #PON power-cycle for every model again: v0.4.3's #EJT-as-grab for the M9207 was WRONG -- on this clone #EJT ejects the disc tray and never grabs, and in fact NO network command can grab the TV (verified live: #POF is a sleep, #PON/#POW only ACK without powering on, and the unit fires CEC One-Touch-Play only on a full IR/remote power-on; the Kodi-side reclaim works fine). Removed the #EJT branch from power_cycle() + the desktop oppo_take_tv() and its now-pointless model selector. On the M9207 Plus switch the TV manually (or via an IR blaster) with grab_tv_on_play=false. 0.4.3 -- M9207 Plus / UDP-203 support (hardware-verified). The OPPO model setting's M9207 option now drives TWO clone-specific behaviours, confirmed live on an M9207 Plus (firmware reports UDP-203): (1) it grabs the TV with a single #EJT instead of the #POF->#PON power-cycle; (2) NFS playback mounts the export ROOT (path_to) and plays the full sub-path under /mnt/nfs1 -- e.g. mount srv/nfs/media -> play /mnt/nfs1/06pr0n/clip.mp4 -- the OPPOSITE of the M9205 model (mount the file's folder, play the bare name). handoff.play() is now model-aware; the default M9205 path is byte-for-byte unchanged. The M9207 file-play was verified end-to-end against real hardware (is_video_playing=True); the disc-folder case follows the same root-mount rule. This is why the add-on previously couldn't hand files to the UDP-203 -- its M9205-only mount-subfolder/bare-name shape was rejected every time. 0.4.2 -- OPPO model selection + an NFS doc/edge fix. New "OPPO model" setting (M9205 / M9207): the M9205 keeps the verified #POF -> #PON power-cycle to grab the TV; the M9207 clone uses a single #EJT instead (operator-reported for that clone -- on a genuine OPPO #EJT is the disc EJECT command, so this is hardware-validated by the operator, not by this code). Wired through the add-on (power_cycle), the in-app settings, and the desktop CEC Switcher (model dropdown); default M9205 = unchanged behaviour. Also corrected the PLAYING_DISCS_FROM_NETWORK guide's checkfolderhasBDMV example (it double-included the mounted folder; the code's /mnt/nfs1/<disc folder> form was already right) and fixed play_bdmv so a disc structure sitting at the export root sends the bare /mnt/nfs1 instead of a dangling /mnt/nfs1/. 82 off-box tests. 0.4.1 -- Second hardening sweep. A multi-agent adversarial hunt found 9 more real bugs (all in error / edge paths); all fixed + pinned by regression tests (78 total). Highlights: the HTTP stop-watcher now uses a tri-state playback probe so a transient network blip is never mistaken for a stop (no premature mid-playback TV reclaim) and is bounded by read-failure + watch-time ceilings so it can never hang and skip the reclaim; serial control maps every failure (missing termios, termios.error, bad baud, absent POSIX flags) to a non-fatal OppoError; #PON still fires when #POF fails; the guided CEC switch-over test is no longer blocked for serial-control users (and Ping reports the serial cable, not a misleading :23 UNREACHABLE); the desktop CEC Switcher surfaces save failures instead of losing settings silently, and treats a connect-then-reset OPPO as reachable. 0.4.0 -- Bug-fix sweep. An adversarial hunt found 13 real bugs, all in previously-untested branches; all fixed + pinned by regression tests (63 total). Highlights: disc-folder mapping for share-root / trailing-slash / .iso-under-BDMV; HTTP status vocabulary (unknown/no-disc tokens now read idle, so the monitor can't loop forever and miss the reclaim); a dropped :23 socket now raises OppoError instead of a raw OSError (so the reclaim and the HTTP fallback aren't skipped); writer/reader profile-path mismatch (both now masterprofile); playercorefactory.xml backup/remove is marker-guarded (never clobbers a user's own file); XML-escaped paths; serial settings wired into the UI; from_addon honours dataclass defaults for undeclared ids; .cec log tag. 0.3.0 -- Clean modular rebuild: detector / handoff / cec / monitor / orchestrator (single-responsibility). The CEC reclaim now goes straight from the orchestrator to Kodi via JSON-RPC -> script.cecreclaim -> CECActivateSource (no flag, no stale-flag class of bug), still single-shot and never re-asserting. New Settings -> Setup & tests: Ping OPPO, Control test (#QPW), and a guided CEC switch-over test. (0.2.0: explicit single-shot reclaim. 0.1.0: fork of v3, IR removed.)
